name: Current Affairs in Indian Economy & TN Dev. Admin. (5): India's FY26 Growth & Global Ranking

sourceRefs.label: tradingeconomics.com/newsonair.gov.in - India's FY26 GDP growth (7.7%, fastest since FY22) and OECD's 'fastest-growing major economy' confirmation; statisticsoftheworld.com - India's 6th nominal-GDP/3rd PPP global ranking (April 2026)

researchNotes: Fifth Economy current-affairs topic (5th lap). GDP figures are inherently somewhat volatile/revision-prone (the lesson from Day 58) — handled here by leading with the STABLE qualitative fact (fastest-growing major economy, confirmed by OECD) and transparently noting that the exact growth-rate figure varies slightly by source (7.7% vs. World Bank's 7.6% estimate) rather than presenting one number as the single certain truth. Expansion pass (Batch 18): added the nominal-GDP ranking's neighbouring countries and why PPP/nominal rankings diverge.

reading.intro: Covers India's FY26 (2025-26) GDP growth performance and its current position among the world's largest economies.

qa.question: What was India's GDP growth rate for the full FY26 (2025-26) financial year, and how does this compare historically?
qa.answer: About 7.7% — the highest since the post-COVID rebound in FY2022. (Note: the World Bank's South Asia Economic Update estimated a close but slightly different 7.6% for the same period — a reminder that GDP estimates vary slightly by source/methodology.)

qa.question: What did an OECD report confirm about India's economic growth status?
qa.answer: India remains the world's FASTEST-GROWING MAJOR ECONOMY, and the fastest-growing economy among G20 countries — holding this position despite US tariff pressures and higher energy costs.

qa.question: What is India's current global ranking by nominal GDP, and by purchasing power parity (PPP), as of April 2026?
qa.answer: 6th largest by nominal GDP (around $4.15 trillion); 3rd largest by purchasing power parity (PPP).

qa.question: What was a key driver of India's strong FY26 growth, per the World Bank?
qa.answer: Strong private consumption, GST rationalization (i.e., GST 2.0, covered in an earlier topic), and export resilience.

qa.question: Which countries are ranked ahead of India in nominal GDP terms (as of April 2026)?
qa.answer: The USA, China, Germany, Japan, and the UK — with India in 6th place just behind them, per the ranking noted in this topic.

qa.question: Why might India's nominal GDP rank (6th) differ so much from its PPP rank (3rd)?
qa.answer: PPP (Purchasing Power Parity) adjusts for the fact that goods/services are cheaper in India relative to countries like the US/Japan — so India's real economic output/purchasing power looks much larger in PPP terms than its currency-converted nominal GDP suggests.

questions.prompt: India's GDP growth for the full FY26 (2025-26) financial year was approximately:
An OECD report confirmed India as:
options: The world's largest economy by nominal GDP

questions.prompt: As of April 2026, India ranks ___ in the world by nominal GDP.
As of April 2026, India ranks ___ in the world by purchasing power parity (PPP).
Per the World Bank, which of these was NOT cited as a key driver of India's FY26 growth?
options: A sharp rise in crude oil prices
questions.explanation: A sharp rise in crude oil prices (higher energy costs were a headwind, not a growth driver).

questions.prompt: India's PPP ranking (3rd) is notably higher than its nominal GDP ranking (6th) mainly because PPP adjusts for:
options: The relative cost of goods/services within each country
questions.explanation: The relative cost of goods/services within each country.
